data.narrative:
Archer Materials is a publicly listed quantum computing company founded by Mohammad Choucair. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage peers: IonQ ($11.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), and Infleqtion ($1.8B).

The peer-median method was applied across this four-company set, yielding a median valuation of $3.8B and an implied valuation range of $1.8B to $11.0B. This approach represents the only triangulation method with sufficient data coverage. The median reflects the midpoint between D-Wave and Rigetti within the comp set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.2B (low) to $4.3B (high), centered on the $3.8B peer median. Archer's current $60M valuation sits substantially below this range, suggesting material upside under peer-comparable terms. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the four direct comparables, path to commercialization, and the volatility evident in the wider peer range ($1.8B–$11.0B spread). The single-method approach and limited peer set restrict confidence in precision; broader market conditions affecting quantum computing equity valuations also present execution risk.
